key_insight: Dynamic LLM-generated reward shaping in cooperative MARL can fail when reward weights change during off-policy replay, so stabilizing the shaping signal is crucial and its benefit depends on the baseline regime.
engineering_relevance: For multi-agent systems using LLMs as reward translators, the paper gives a practical recipe—freeze or smooth reward weights—to improve training stability without breaking experience replay.
method: Phase-based freeze schedule to keep reward shaping stationary within each training phase.
Exponential moving average (EMA) smoothing to limit per-episode drift in LLM-generated reward weights.
Regime analysis across augmentative, essential, and supplementary settings using QMIX, with an exploratory VDN extension.
evidence_signal: Across three cooperative environments and five random seeds, EMA lifted Simple Spread success from 74.4% to 86.7% and rescued Level-Based Foraging from 0.1% to 95.9%, while unstabilized updates could collapse performance to 15.2%.
watch_item: Dynamic reward shaping must respect stationarity in off-policy replay; otherwise stored transitions may become inconsistent with current reward weights and destabilize learning.
paper_title: Regime-Conditional Stabilisation of LLM-Augmented Cooperative Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
